description: The house-style good memo: a grounded REJECT arguing verbatim from the quant record (momentum-v1 FAIL, xsmom-pit PASS but name outside the winner decile), observable kill criteria, a genuine dissent, and a diverse bull/bear debate. Every metric should pass.

memo.thesis: The committee rejects AVGO for new capital. The quant record is decisive: momentum-v1 failed its decision gates on real data with 0 of 4 folds passing, and while xsmom-pit holds a decision-grade PASS, AVGO sits outside the winner decile, so no validated strategy covers the name. Trend strength above both moving averages is noted but is not, by itself, a deployable edge.
kill_criteria: AVGO enters the xsmom-pit winner decile at a monthly rebalance while the family PASS verdict still stands
The twenty-session return turns negative and the close falls below both the SMA20 and SMA50 levels
memo.dissent: Strongest case against rejection: the desk is dodging a name its own point-in-time machinery is close to selecting, and a standing REJECT that flips at the next rebalance whipsaws the watchlist process while surrendering the entry that persistent trend structure already flags.
memo.debate_summary: The sides disagreed genuinely: the bull rested on trend persistence, the bear on the absence of validated coverage; the bear point that AVGO sits outside the winner decile went unanswered and carried the memo.

evidence: AVGO daily closes (EODHD vendor bars, split-adjusted): latest close 172.35 on 2026-07-10, previous close 170.10, 20 sessions ago 158.32. Window: 60 sessions ending 2026-07-10.
DCP indicators for AVGO as of 2026-07-10: SMA20 168.40, SMA50 161.75, 20-day return 8.86 percent, last close 172.35.

Quant validation record as of 2026-07-10: family momentum-v1 FAILED decision gates on real data (null-model p 0.830, deflated Sharpe negative, 0 of 4 walk-forward folds passed); family xsmom-pit holds a decision-grade PASS on the S&P 500 point-in-time universe (4 of 4 folds, null p 0.000, deflated Sharpe 0.998). AVGO is not in the current xsmom-pit winner decile; no validated strategy currently covers AVGO.

strongest_points: Price has held above both moving averages for the whole sixty-session window, a persistence rarely seen without institutional accumulation
The twenty-session return is strongly positive while the broader tape has been flat, showing genuine relative strength
The xsmom-pit family PASS proves the fund can validate momentum-style edges, and AVGO exhibits exactly that profile
bull.weakest_opposing_point: The bear leans entirely on gate verdicts and ignores that validation lags the tape by construction
bull.concede: No validated strategy currently covers the name, so any entry today rides an ungated thesis.

strongest_points: momentum-v1 failed every decision gate on real data, and the graveyard of failed variants is exactly why the fund gates strategies
AVGO is outside the xsmom-pit winner decile, so the one validated family the fund owns declines to select it
Buying trend strength without validated coverage is style drift the mandate forbids
bear.weakest_opposing_point: The bull's accumulation story is inference from price alone with no flow evidence anywhere in the corpus
bear.concede: The trend structure is real and has persisted for the full window.
